title: [epic] Lazy state synchronization wire — server-side implementation per ADR-0018
description:
Implement ADR-0018 (Lazy state synchronization). Wire shape is unchanged; semantics of TERMINAL_OUTPUT.bytes shift from "PTY pass-through" to "minimum VT to transition consumer's last-acked state to canonical's current state".

Scope per ADR-0018 addendum (2026-05-26 revision): the per-consumer cache primitive is libghostty RenderState, already used in-tree. The work is lifecycle (per-attached-consumer RenderState ownership in TerminalActor) + incremental synthesis (extend SnapshotSynthesizer) + tick scheduler + ack-driven dirty reset. Estimated ~200-400 LOC of new code plus per-consumer cache management.

Blocked on phux-28f (TerminalActor placement) for the lifecycle piece. The synthesis extension can proceed in parallel against the existing SnapshotSynthesizer.

References:
- ADR-0018 (Lazy state synchronization) + 2026-05-26 addendum
- research/2026-05-26-state-sync-algorithm.md (revised Dependencies §)
- bd memory: renderstate-as-cache, mosh-decomposition (updated)
close_reason: Core state-sync stack shipped: q0e.1 (incremental synthesizer #4), q0e.2 (per-consumer RenderState lifecycle #3), q0e.3 (tick scheduler #6), q0e.4 (FRAME_ACK handler closing the loop #7). Plus phux-l0t (libghostty FFI bug diagnosis #5) and phux-6jb (tracking ticket for upstream fix revisit). Only q0e.5 (RTT-adaptive tick) remains, deferred per its description. ADR-0018 fully implemented end-to-end.

title: client: attach failure wedges terminal (alt-screen + cursor hidden); graceful failure UX
description:
Repro: \`phux attach <session>\` when no server is running, or when the session doesn't exist, or when the client gets signal-killed mid-attach. Terminal is left in alt-screen mode + cursor hidden + sometimes raw mode. User has to type \`reset\` blind.

Root causes:
1. \`attach::run\` installs RawModeGuard (alt-screen + raw mode + hide cursor) BEFORE the UDS connect + handshake. Any pre-handshake failure flickers the terminal through alt-screen for nothing.
2. RawModeGuard::Drop only runs on Rust unwinding. SIGTERM/SIGKILL/SIGHUP bypass it. The cleanup escapes (\\x1b[?1049l + \\x1b[?25h) never get written.
3. No signal handler installed to restore terminal state on signal exit.
4. Auto-spawn server always uses DEFAULT_SESSION_NAME ('default') but client asks for whatever was on the CLI. Mismatch causes attach to fail at handshake while client is already in alt-screen.
5. Connection errors surface as 'attach failed: connection refused' — actionable would be 'no phux server at <path>; start one with \`phux server\`' with the exact suggested command.
6. Stale socket files survive a crashed server (separate ticket — file follow-up).

Acceptance for THIS ticket:
- attach::run defers RawModeGuard installation until AFTER handshake + ATTACH succeeds (or skip alt-screen entirely on early failure).
- SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers installed that run terminal cleanup before exit.
- ConnectionRefused/NotFound socket errors produce a one-line clean error WITHOUT entering alt-screen.
- Auto-spawn passes the user's requested session name through to the spawned server.
- Test under the new \`Screen\` harness: a synthetic 'no server' scenario captures stdout and asserts NO alt-screen-enter byte sequence appears.

Out-of-scope: stale-socket cleanup (file follow-up bd if not already covered).

title: feat(server): wire INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/INPUT_FOCUS dispatch in handle_client
description: handle_client in runtime.rs falls through input frames to the catch-all _ arm, silently dropping every keystroke from attached clients. Wire FrameKind::InputKey/InputMouse/InputFocus to the existing PaneActor input mpsc via ServerState. Add a wire-level integration test that drives an InputKey frame through a real UDS connection and verifies it reaches the PaneActor PTY (not just direct PaneActor exercise like pty_pump.rs). Validation: drop with warn! on unknown pane_id or unattached client. Closes the gap noted in phux-byc.6.5 description.
close_reason: Closed

title: byc.8: server-side Terminal placement — spawn_local + LocalSet OR actor pattern
description:
Coming concern for byc.8 (server ATTACH handler). libghostty Terminal is !Send + !Sync (holds NonNull<TerminalImpl>, no unsafe impl Send/Sync in libghostty source).

runtime.rs:258 currently uses tokio::spawn (Send-required) for per-client handler tasks. Today the handler holds no Terminal — only logs unhandled frames. byc.8 will add real ATTACH handling that needs to own a Terminal per pane.

Three viable shapes — byc.8 must pick one:

1. spawn_local + LocalSet — keeps current_thread runtime per ADR-0003; Terminal stays on the runtime thread; preferred.
2. Dedicated pane-supervisor task that owns the Terminal; channels (mpsc) for input/output. The 'actor model' that was flagged earlier in the session as a possible refactor for ServerState shape. Cleaner separation but more plumbing.
3. RefCell<Terminal> behind Rc on a single-threaded executor. Discouraged — fights tokio's spawn conventions.

Do NOT try Arc<Mutex<Terminal>> — won't compile (Mutex requires Send for cross-thread sharing; Terminal isn't Send).

Decision needed at start of byc.8 work. ADR-worthy if the actor pattern is picked.
close_reason: Resolved by ADR-0014 (per-terminal TerminalActor on LocalSet) + TerminalActor impl (crates/phux-server/src/terminal_actor.rs, 987 LOC, uses spawn_local + LocalSet per design 3 in original ticket). The ticket was filed when the actor pattern was still being chosen; ADR-0014 picked option 1 (spawn_local + LocalSet, one borrower, no RefCell) and shipped as TerminalActor in phux-byc.5. Unblocks phux-q0e.2.

title: Per-client capability downsampling: define ColorSupport + downsample fn + per-client storage
description:
SPEC §6.2 conformance: `crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs:121-122` unconditionally
emits `Color::Rgb` with a comment that says "the wire carries the final pixel
value" — directly violating SPEC §6.2: "a client advertising Indexed256 MUST
never receive truecolor RGB cells; the server must downsample."

## Design seam: per-pane capture vs per-client fanout

Three options were considered:

1. First-attach floor — capture in the lowest tier any current client
   advertises. Rejected: locks downsampling into the canonical state; a
   later-attaching TrueColor client cannot recover precision.
2. Per-tier cache — capture once in TrueColor, materialize one cached
   diff per active tier. Rejected as premature; complicates invalidation
   and is unnecessary until profiling shows it.
3. On-demand downsample at fanout — capture once in canonical TrueColor;
   each per-client fanout call downsamples to that client's advertised
   tier. Selected. Matches the existing "server owns canonical state,
   clients see derived views" model and keeps the diff-compute path tier-
   agnostic.

## Scope of THIS ticket (substrate only)

- `ColorSupport` enum (TrueColor | Indexed256 | Indexed16) in phux-protocol.
- `downsample(Color, ColorSupport) -> Color` (as helper fn, since `Color` is a
  re-export of libghostty-vt's StyleColor — no inherent impl possible).
- Per-client cap storage on `AttachedClient` in phux-server::state.
  Defaulted to `ColorSupport::TrueColor` (most-permissive; clients without
  advertised caps don't get downgraded).
- New `phux-server::downsample` module with `downsample_op` /
  `downsample_cell` for byc.5's fanout to plug into.
- Comprehensive unit + property tests.
- `diff_spike` MUST remain byte-identical — downsample is per-fanout, not
  per-capture; nothing in the capture path changes.

## Out of scope (follow-up tickets)

- HELLO ColorSupport wiring (SPEC §6.1 conformance — separate task).
- CIELAB perceptual color distance for downsample (better Indexed16 picks).
- Fanout layer itself (byc.5).
close_reason: Substrate landed in cc30ab5. ColorSupport enum + downsample_color + per-client color_support on AttachedClient + phux-server::downsample helper module + comprehensive tests (unit + proptest). diff_spike byte-identical. Follow-ups: phux-7lf (HELLO wiring), phux-i02 (CIELAB), phux-982 (Cow signature).

title: Wire frames: ATTACH/ATTACHED/DETACH/DETACHED + INPUT_KEY/MOUSE/FOCUS/PASTE
description:
phux-byc.4 surfaced that the message-catalog frames from SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9 are missing from phux-protocol's FrameKind enum. byc.4 landed the server-side state scaffold but its 5 wire-level integration tests had to be deferred until these variants exist. Until this lands, the byc epic cannot demonstrate end-to-end attach. Likely belongs as a phux-6yl follow-up; filing standalone for visibility.

## Required Skills
rust,tlv,protocol-design
design:
phux-protocol::wire::frame::FrameKind currently has: Hello, Ping, PaneDiff.

Add (per SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9):

  C → S
    Attach { session_name: String, role: ?? }   // §7.1
    Detach                                       // §7.3
    InputKey(KeyEvent)                           // §9.1
    InputMouse(MouseEvent)                       // §9.2
    InputFocus(FocusEvent)                       // §9.3
    InputPaste(PasteEvent)                       // §9.4
    InputRaw(Bytes)                              // §9.5 (only if a passthrough is needed; can defer)

  S → C
    Attached { session_id, window_id, pane_id, snapshot: PaneSnapshot }   // §7.2
    Detached                                                              // §7.3
    Bell { pane_id }                                                       // §7.6
    OscEvent { ... }                                                       // §7.7 (defer; not needed for byc.5)
    Alert { ... }                                                          // §7.8 (defer)

Plus the SessionId tagged-union on the wire (per ADR-0007 §3): protocol-level
SessionId is u32 newtype; on-wire it's tagged with the addressed satellite.

For each frame:
  - Add a FrameKind variant
  - Add TLV field IDs in wire/field.rs (per phux-6yl.4)
  - Add encoder + decoder, mirroring how PaneDiff is wired
  - Add an insta hex snapshot covering at least one representative payload
  - Round-trip proptest if the variant has structured payload

phux_protocol::ids::SessionId (wire-side u32) vs phux_core::ids::SessionId
(slotmap key, opaque). They are distinct by design — wire SessionIds are
allocated by the server, slotmap keys are an in-process registry detail.
Add a server-side ::from(phux_core::ids::SessionId) -> wire-side SessionId
mapping + reverse lookup. Don't define a `From` impl in phux-protocol
itself (would force a phux-core dep on phux-protocol).

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol
  cargo run -p phux-server --example diff_spike   # untouched
acceptance_criteria: FrameKind has all the C→S and S→C variants from SPEC §7. Round-trip + insta snapshots cover each new variant. phux-server's deferred byc.4 integration tests now compile (filing a separate ticket for them).
notes: Discovered by phux-byc.4 agent retro. Blocks byc.5 (e2e test), 9gw.3 (attach loop), and the e2e harness epic phux-a87.
close_reason: Landed in fa335e1. Added FrameKind variants Attach/Detach/InputKey/InputMouse/InputFocus/InputPaste (C->S) and Attached/Detached/Bell (S->C) per SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9.1-§9.4. Encoders/decoders mirror PaneDiff pattern, 13 insta snapshots + 7 proptest round-trips. Non-obvious decisions for byc.6: (1) AttachRole {Primary=0, Viewer=1} is phux-defined (not in SPEC §13) -- minimum useful split. (2) PaneSnapshot {cols, rows, ops: Vec<DiffOp>} is minimum per SPEC §8.4 -- cursor/modes/scrollback are TODO. (3) Detached carries no body this wave (SPEC §7.3 {reason, message} deferred). (4) FrameKind dropped Eq (now PartialEq only) because MouseEvent has f64. (5) Added DecodeError::UnknownEnumValue {field, value} for libghostty atom round-trip safety. NOT touched: phux-core, phux-server (per task scope), no From<phux_core::ids::SessionId>. Deferred: OscEvent §7.7, Alert §7.8, InputRaw §9.5. All quality gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, nextest (67 protocol + 204 workspace), diff_spike, cargo deny.

title: From impls for all four input event types + server-side KeyEncoder pipeline
description: In phux-server: implement field-for-field conversion from phux_protocol::input::{KeyEvent, MouseEvent, FocusEvent, PasteEvent} to the corresponding libghostty_vt::{key, mouse, focus, paste}::Event types. Wire the key encoder pipeline: per-pane KeyEncoder, set_options_from_terminal(&term) before each encode, encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf). The encoder lives server-side; the wire never carries encoder options (ADR-0006). Mouse/Focus/Paste have their own libghostty encoders that follow the same pattern.
design:
New module: crates/phux-server/src/input/
  key.rs    — impl From<&phux_protocol::input::KeyEvent> for libghostty_vt::key::Event + PerPaneKeyEncoder
  mouse.rs  — impl From for MouseEvent + per-pane mouse encoder
  focus.rs  — impl From for FocusEvent + DEC 1004 gating
  paste.rs  — impl From for PasteEvent + bracketed-paste decision
  mod.rs    — re-exports

PerPaneKeyEncoder holds a libghostty_vt::key::KeyEncoder + reusable Vec<u8>. Calling sites: `encoder.set_options_from_terminal(&term); encoder.encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf)`. Mouse/focus follow the same shape.

Encoder lives server-side. Never serialized to wire. See ADR-0006 rationale.

Conversion is infallible (libghostty event types are supersets / equal-sets of ours).
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: round-trip a KeyEvent through the From impl and assert all fields preserved. Conversion is infallible (no Result).
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:783-818 — §9.1.6 Server-side key encoding pipeline
  SPEC.md:871-878 — §9.2.2 Server-side mouse encoding pipeline

title: Server pane plumbing: PTY + per-pane diff loop
description:
Wire a real pane: spawn child PTY, feed bytes into libghostty_vt::Terminal, run grid::capture each frame, compute DiffOps, gate by FRAME_ACK/snapshot fallback per SPEC §6. Pools RenderState/RowIterator/CellIterator per pane (the spike allocates each call). Single-pane, no IPC yet — that's phux-byc.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `cargo run -p phux-server --example one_pane bash` spawns a real shell, accepts typed input from stdin, prints DiffOp summaries to stderr per frame.
- [ ] Steady-state capture loop allocates zero bytes (validated by criterion bench or dhat-rs heap trace).
- [ ] Clean shutdown on child exit (no zombie process, no panic on EOF).
- [ ] diff_spike example continues to print "replay invariant: snap0 + diff(0→1) == snap1   ✓" after the refactor.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-server` green; clippy -D warnings clean.
close_reason: bc1.1 + bc1.2 landed via waves 3+4

title: Protocol foundation: input types + wire codec
description:
Translate SPEC §9 (input) and Appendix A (TLV framing) into Rust types and a length-prefixed codec. Closes the loop between phux_protocol and libghostty_vt by establishing the From<&Event> for libghostty_vt::*::Event boundary, with round-trip snapshot tests. Blocks all wire-format-dependent work (server runtime, client, transport).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A KeyEvent constructed in phux-protocol, converted via the server-side From impl, encoded by libghostty_vt's KeyEncoder, produces VT bytes that match committed insta hex snapshots for ≥10 representative cases (incl. ≥3 KIP enhancement levels).
- [ ] Arbitrary `Vec<DiffOp>` round-trips through the wire codec via proptest (≥1024 cases, no shrinks to failure).
- [ ] Insta hex-dump snapshots committed for ≥8 representative diff frames (small change visible in PR diff).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green; `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean.
- [ ] No libghostty types leak through phux-protocol's public API (verified by `cargo tree -p phux-protocol` not listing libghostty-vt).
close_reason: all 7 subtasks landed via waves 1-3

title: Client: split-pane/kill-pane wire-up + SIGWINCH per-Terminal RESIZE
description:
Filed during phux-4li.10 integration (33eb147 landed the wire layer).

Wire frames are available in phux-protocol. This ticket replaces the warn+bell stubs in attach::driver::run_action with real frame I/O and finishes the SIGWINCH wire-emission half of phux-4li.9.

Scope:

1. **split-pane dispatcher.** In crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs run_action:
   - Replace the warn+bell stub for 'split-pane' with: allocate a request_id (next_request_id wrapping_add); emit SPAWN_TERMINAL{request_id, collection: DEFAULT_COLLECTION_ID, command: None, cwd: <inherit-focused — for v0.1 use None until phux-4li.1's CWD inheritance lands>, env: None}; record (request_id → pending split intent {focused, dir}).
   - On TERMINAL_SPAWNED{request_id, result: Ok(new_terminal_id)}: look up the pending intent, apply attach::actions::apply_split(state, new_terminal_id, dir), update layout_state, lazily insert PaneSlot for new_terminal_id, emit SetMetadata for the new envelope, repaint.
   - On TERMINAL_SPAWNED{request_id, result: Err(_)}: log + bell + clear pending intent.

2. **kill-pane dispatcher.** Replace the warn+bell stub with:
   - Emit a kill-frame. (Server-side kill comes via PTY exit naturally; in v0.1 just write 'exit\n' to the focused Terminal as a soft-kill, or use whatever explicit-kill frame the L1 family provides. If no explicit kill exists today, document as a known limitation and use the soft-kill approach.)
   - On TERMINAL_CLOSED{terminal_id}: call attach::actions::apply_kill(state) and reconcile.

3. **SIGWINCH per-pane TERMINAL_RESIZE emission.** In the SIGWINCH arm of main_loop (recently rewritten by phux-4li.9):
   - The block that today computes  and has the  pending marker.
   - Replace with: for (terminal_id, new_rect) in &diff.changed: conn.send(&terminal_resize_frame(terminal_id, new_rect.w, new_rect.h)).await?; — using a new construct helper similar to viewport_resize_frame.

4. **TERMINAL_CLOSED handler.** New arm in handle_server_frame for FrameKind::TerminalClosed: if the closed terminal is in layout_state, fold it out via apply_kill, drop the PaneSlot from panes, repaint. If the focus pane just closed, pick a sane new focus (sibling preferred).

5. **Tests.** Unit tests covering:
   - run_action 'split-pane' allocates a request_id, emits SPAWN_TERMINAL, parks the intent.
   - TERMINAL_SPAWNED Ok with matching id applies the split, emits SetMetadata.
   - TERMINAL_SPAWNED Err with matching id rings the bell.
   - SIGWINCH path emits one TERMINAL_RESIZE per changed pane (proptest tied to ReflowDiff.changed).

NOT in scope:
- CWD inheritance (phux-4li.1).
- Drag-on-border resize (deferred).
- 'phux new' subcommand (phux-k61.x).

Blocks: phux-4li epic success criteria 'prefix + | splits' and 'prefix + x kills' working end-to-end.
Depends on: phux-4li.10.1 (server handler must be able to reply for these flows to round-trip).
Acceptance: split-pane + kill-pane work end-to-end against a real phux-server with phux-4li.10.1 landed. cargo nextest run -p phux-client passes.
close_reason: Landed in 47eebb2. run_action 'split-pane' allocates request_id + parks PendingSplit{focused_at_request, dir} keyed by request_id + emits SPAWN_TERMINAL. TerminalSpawned handler drains pending + calls pure-seam apply_spawned_ok (anchors to focused_at_request even if live focus moved). 'kill-pane' soft-kills via 5-frame INPUT_KEY 'exit\n' sequence; TerminalClosed handler drives apply_terminal_closed (pure seam wrapping actions::apply_kill). SIGWINCH multi-pane branch now emits one TERMINAL_RESIZE per ReflowDiff.changed pane. 9 new unit tests including the focused_at_request regression. 699 tests total green.

title: Server: SPAWN_TERMINAL handler + TERMINAL_CLOSED emit + TERMINAL_RESIZE TIOCSWINSZ
description:
Filed during phux-4li.10 integration (33eb147 landed the wire layer).

Wire frames are allocated and round-trip clean in phux-protocol; this ticket implements the server-side handlers.

Scope:

1. **SPAWN_TERMINAL handler.** On receipt:
   - Look up the named CollectionId; if absent reply TERMINAL_SPAWNED{request_id, result: Err(CollectionNotFound)}.
   - Spawn a new Terminal under that Collection. Reuse the seed_session_with_actor / seed_session_with_pty helpers that resolve_create_if_missing (phux-k61.3, e9242fe) already exercises. command/cwd/env from the SPAWN frame populate the spawn (Option<Vec<String>> command of None means default shell per ServerConfig).
   - On success reply TERMINAL_SPAWNED{request_id, result: Ok(terminal_id)}. The Terminal is now part of the Collection; subscribed clients receive normal TERMINAL_OUTPUT/SNAPSHOT for it.
   - On spawn failure reply TERMINAL_SPAWNED{request_id, result: Err(SpawnFailed(reason))}.

2. **TERMINAL_CLOSED emit.** Wire it through whichever PTY-exit detection path the server already has (grep for child process reaping / waitpid / read-loop EOF). When a Terminal's PTY exits, emit TERMINAL_CLOSED{terminal_id, exit_status} to every client subscribed to that Terminal's Collection. exit_status is the WEXITSTATUS if normal, None if killed by signal.

3. **TERMINAL_RESIZE handler.** On receipt:
   - Look up the Terminal; if absent log + drop (the wire frame has no reply variant by design).
   - Call ioctl(TIOCSWINSZ) on the PTY's master FD with the new cols/rows. Also resize libghostty's server-side Terminal (mirrors what the client does in repaint_multi_pane). 

4. **Tests.** Integration tests in crates/phux-server/tests/ following the metadata_reply / attach_create_if_missing pattern:
   - SPAWN_TERMINAL{name=existing Collection} → TERMINAL_SPAWNED Ok with a fresh TerminalId; subsequent INPUT_KEY to that id is delivered to the new PTY.
   - SPAWN_TERMINAL{name=unknown Collection} → TERMINAL_SPAWNED Err(CollectionNotFound).
   - Spawn a Terminal with command=Some(['/bin/false']) → PTY exits → TERMINAL_CLOSED arrives with exit_status=Some(non-zero).
   - TERMINAL_RESIZE{cols, rows} → re-attached client observes the new dims in TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT.

NOT in scope:
- Process-group preservation across reattach.
- Capability checking (assume L1 always; the wire frames are L1).

Blocks: phux-4li.10.2 (client wire-up depends on server being able to reply). 4li epic 'split-pane works end-to-end' criterion.
Acceptance: above tests green; cargo nextest run -p phux-server passes.
close_reason: Landed in 45b7c84. handle_spawn_terminal + handle_terminal_resize handlers in runtime.rs::handle_client. broadcast_terminal_closed fires from spawn_terminal_exit_watcher. Reused seed_session_with_pty + TerminalHandle.resize mpsc (which already drives libghostty Terminal::resize + portable_pty MasterPty.resize for TIOCSWINSZ). exit_notify oneshot widened from () to Option<i32>. Spawn handler auto-subscribes the spawning client to the new Terminal (server policy choice, not wire change). 4/4 acceptance tests landed without #[ignore]. Wire-shape friction: portable_pty signal-kill vs exit-code distinguished via Display parsing — documented in exit_status_to_wire.

title: SPAWN/TerminalClosed wire frames: unblock 4li.5 split-pane + kill-pane I/O
description:
Filed during phux-4li.5 integration (78d224d), expanded during phux-4li.9 wire-up.

phux-4li.5 landed action dispatch; phux-4li.9 wired SIGWINCH reflow. Both surface the same wire gap: the L1 Terminal lifecycle is missing three frames the daily-drive arc needs.

Missing wire frames (allocate in SPEC §7.2 L1):

1. **SPAWN_TERMINAL (C→S)**: {request_id: u32, collection: CollectionId, command: Option<Vec<String>>, cwd: Option<String>, env: Option<Vec<(String, String)>>}
2. **TERMINAL_SPAWNED (S→C reply)**: {request_id: u32, terminal_id: TerminalId} OR error variant
3. **TERMINAL_CLOSED (S→C push)**: {terminal_id: TerminalId, exit_status: Option<i32>}
4. **TERMINAL_RESIZE (C→S)**: {terminal_id: TerminalId, cols: u16, rows: u16} — per-Terminal PTY resize when a client-side split changes a pane's geometry. Today client only sends VIEWPORT_RESIZE (outer viewport); the server has no way to learn each Terminal's per-pane dims, so PTYs receive stale TIOCSWINSZ values. Surfaced during phux-4li.9 SIGWINCH wire-up.

Why this matters:
- split-pane / kill-pane are stuck at warn+bell in phux-4li.5's dispatcher (no SPAWN/TerminalClosed).
- After a SIGWINCH or a split, every pane's libghostty Terminal is resized client-side (phux-4li.9), but the SERVER's PTYs are not — shells inside see stale (cols, rows) until VIEWPORT_RESIZE happens to coincide.
- (Server-side session creation does exist via AttachTarget::CreateIfMissing landed in phux-k61.3 — the building blocks for spawning Terminals are there; they just aren't reachable through a dedicated post-attach SPAWN frame.)

Scope:
1. Allocate 4 discriminants in SPEC §7.2 (L1). Document field layouts.
2. Encode/decode + insta snapshots + proptest round-trips in phux-protocol.
3. Server: handle SPAWN_TERMINAL by creating a Terminal under the named Collection, reusing the same session-creation primitives that resolve_create_if_missing already exercises. Emit TERMINAL_CLOSED when a PTY exits. Handle TERMINAL_RESIZE by calling ioctl(TIOCSWINSZ) on the named PTY.
4. Client wire-up in attach::driver: 
   - Replace the warn+bell stubs in run_action for split-pane and kill-pane with real SPAWN_TERMINAL emission + correlation on TERMINAL_SPAWNED + TERMINAL_CLOSED-driven kill_pane fold.
   - In the SIGWINCH handler (phux-4li.9), iterate ReflowDiff.changed and emit one TERMINAL_RESIZE per changed pane. Replace the `let _ = diff.changed` pending marker.
5. End-to-end test: a client issues split-pane → server allocates → TERMINAL_SPAWNED arrives → layout grows → SET_METADATA fires → server confirms via METADATA_CHANGED → next paint shows two panes. Resize the outer terminal → TERMINAL_RESIZE emitted per pane → PTYs see updated dims.

NOT in scope:
- Multi-collection routing (v0.1 uses DEFAULT_COLLECTION_ID).
- Process-group preservation across reattach (separate concern).
- 'phux new' subcommand surface (k61 family).

Blocks: phux-4li (epic success criteria 'prefix + | splits' and 'prefix + x kills'); finishes the wire-emission half of phux-4li.9.
Acceptance: split-pane and kill-pane work end-to-end in an attached client; SIGWINCH triggers per-pane TIOCSWINSZ. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Wire layer landed in 33eb147. Allocated 0x22 SPAWN_TERMINAL + 0x23 TERMINAL_RESIZE (C->S) and 0xA1 TERMINAL_CLOSED + 0xA2 TERMINAL_SPAWNED (S->C). SpawnError #[non_exhaustive] enum with CollectionNotFound/SpawnFailed(String); SpawnResult tagged Ok=0x00/Err=0x01 documented as the Result<T,E> wire convention in SPEC §7.2.1. 8 insta snapshots + 4 proptest round-trips + 6 hand-rolled edge cases. No phux-server / phux-client touches needed (existing FrameKind matches have catch-all arms). Server + client wire-up filed as 4li.10.1 + 4li.10.2.

title: SIGWINCH wire-up: call attach::reflow::compute_reflow + emit RESIZE frames
description:
Tiny follow-up from wave-B fan-out (4li.4 + 4li.7 landed disjoint).

phux-4li.4 grew the driver's main_loop to hold N panes with a layout_state field. phux-4li.7 shipped a pure attach::reflow::compute_reflow(...) function with tests. The wire-up — calling compute_reflow on SIGWINCH and emitting RESIZE frames for each (TerminalId, Rect) in ReflowDiff.changed — was intentionally split out to avoid a driver.rs collision during the 3-wide fan-out.

Scope:
- In crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs main_loop's SIGWINCH arm: replace the current 'render focused pane only' behavior with:
  1. let prev_rects = compute current pane_rects from previous viewport (cache this on the driver struct, or recompute from prev viewport)
  2. let diff = attach::reflow::compute_reflow(&layout_state, &prev_rects, new_outer_dims)
  3. for (terminal_id, new_rect) in diff.changed: send RESIZE { terminal_id, cols: new_rect.w, rows: new_rect.h }; also call panes[terminal_id].terminal.resize(...)
  4. if diff.too_small: log warning, continue (no panic per ADR-0019 decision 5)
  5. Trigger a full repaint via attach::multi_pane::compute_layout + paint_dividers + per-pane render_at
- Driver gains a prev_viewport: (u16, u16) field (or equivalent) to track the previous outer dims.
- Test: unit test that a SIGWINCH triggers RESIZE only for panes whose (w, h) actually changed (uses the reflow ReflowDiff.changed set directly).

NOT in scope:
- VIEWPORT_RESIZE wire frame path (phux-4hp).
- Min-size freezing (ADR-0019 punts).

Acceptance:
- Manually resizing an attached terminal under a 2-pane split reflows both panes proportionally.
- cargo nextest run -p phux-client green.
close_reason: Landed in next commit. SIGWINCH arm of attach driver now branches on layout_state.tree.is_some(): multi-pane path computes prev_rects via multi_pane::compute_layout(prev_dims), runs reflow::compute_reflow for too_small detection, then calls existing repaint_multi_pane helper (which clears screen + libghostty resize per pane + paints dividers + status bar). Single-pane fallback preserved. Wire-side per-Terminal RESIZE emission gated on phux-4li.10 (expanded to include TERMINAL_RESIZE alongside SPAWN/TERMINAL_CLOSED frame work).

title: L3 metadata: GET/LIST wire reply path (blocks 4li.5 reconcile-on-attach)
description:
Follow-up filed during phux-4li.2 integration (d74beda).

phux-4li.2 allocated GET_METADATA (0x50) and LIST_METADATA (0x53) discriminants, and the server *executes* the K/V operations in-process, but the wire reply path was deferred: there is currently no envelope that carries a GET response (the requested value, or LIST's key list) back to the requesting client.

Why this matters:
- METADATA_CHANGED carries value inline (set during 4li.2), so steady-state push works.
- BUT on initial attach, a client has no way to fetch the current layout blob from L3 metadata — it must wait for the next SET to learn anything. That breaks reattach-restores-layout (one of the phux-4li epic's success criteria).
- phux-4li.5 (action dispatch) reconciles its layout mirror against METADATA_CHANGED, but the *initial* GET to seed the mirror has no return path.

Scope:
- Decide between (a) standing up a generic COMMAND_RESULT envelope (per SPEC §11 sketch) that correlates by request_id, OR (b) shipping a dedicated METADATA_VALUE S→C reply frame keyed by request_id. Trade-offs in an ADR addendum if (a).
- Wire it: encode/decode + snapshots + proptest round-trip + integration test (GET via the server's K/V actor returns the expected bytes).
- LIST replies: same envelope, returning Vec<String> of key names only (consistent with 4li.2's ticket scope).

NOT in scope:
- Per-key size cap enforcement (separate cleanup).
- CAS / optimistic locking.

Blocks: phux-4li.5 (action dispatch needs reconcile-on-attach).
Acceptance: A client can GET_METADATA(scope=Collection(default), key='phux.tui.layout/v1') after attach and receive the current value over the wire. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Landed in 54aad83. Dedicated METADATA_VALUE (0xD1) + METADATA_KEYS (0xD2) S->C reply frames keyed by request_id (echoes the GET/LIST request). Rationale: COMMAND_RESULT would force premature design closure on L1/L2 payloads; L3 family already opinionated on inline values per 4li.2. Server reply path L3-gated. SPEC §7.4 updated.

title: phux ls subcommand — list sessions
description: Extract a proper `phux ls` subcommand (and the `list-sessions` alias per DESIGN.md §1). Today the naked-phux dispatcher (phux-k61.1) uses a hardcoded fallback cascade (AttachTarget::Last → AttachTarget::ByName("default")) precisely because no client-side session-listing path exists. Adding `phux ls` will need either a new wire frame (LIST_SESSIONS / SessionList response) OR a generic COMMAND request/response per SPEC §14. Coordinate with phux-byc.* IPC work — adding a frame here without consulting that epic risks divergent shapes.

title: Fix private intra-doc links in phux-client/predict
description:
After phux-60l wires `cargo doc` into `just ci`, `just ci` (and the GH CI doc job on main) fails on two private intra-doc links introduced by phux-9gw (predict widen / per-cell reconcile):

- `crates/phux-client/src/predict/mod.rs:97` — `[`reconcile`]` links to private `mod reconcile`.
- `crates/phux-client/src/predict/state.rs:63` — `[`super::reconcile`]` likewise.

GH CI `doc` job is currently red on main because of these. Fix: de-link the references (replace with plain backticks) or surface the items publicly. Trivial.
close_reason: Landed in commit fixing private intra-doc links in predict/mod.rs and predict/state.rs. cargo doc -D warnings now green on main.

title: Resize / SIGWINCH reflow under splits: recompute Rects, emit per-pane RESIZE on dim change
description:
Per ADR-0019 decision 5: when SIGWINCH fires (or VIEWPORT_RESIZE arrives — phux-4hp territory), recompute divider counts, re-run pane_rects with (cols - h_dividers, rows - v_dividers), and emit RESIZE { terminal_id, cols, rows } only for panes whose dimensions actually changed. Split ratios are preserved.

Scope:
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs SIGWINCH handler (currently a no-op per the module doc) gains the reflow path.
- pane_rects is called against the new outer dims; the diff against the previous Rect set determines which Terminals get a RESIZE frame.
- The render path (phux-4li.4) reads new Rects on next paint.
- If the new viewport is too small to fit the layout (any pane would be < 2 cols or < 1 row), log a warning and render garbage; do not panic. v0.1 has no min-size freezing per ADR-0019.

NOT in scope:
- Min-size freezing (DESIGN.md §6.2 design intent; ADR-0019 punts).
- VIEWPORT_RESIZE wire frame work — that's phux-4hp. This ticket only handles local SIGWINCH; when phux-4hp lands the same reflow code is called from the VIEWPORT_RESIZE path.

Acceptance:
- Resizing the outer terminal from 80x24 to 120x30 with a 2-pane vertical split recomputes both panes' widths in proportion to the preserved ratio.
- Only panes whose dims changed receive a RESIZE frame (verify with a unit test against a controlled LayoutState).
- Shrinking below viable size logs a warning and does not crash.
- cargo nextest run -p phux-client green.
acceptance_criteria: SIGWINCH reflows pane Rects, preserves ratios, emits RESIZE only for changed panes. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Landed in 5fe148e. Pure attach::reflow module with compute_reflow(LayoutState, prev_rects, new_dims) -> ReflowDiff{new_rects, changed, too_small}. Driver wire-up deferred per narrowed scope — see follow-up ticket.

title: Input routing: route stdin and input frames to focused pane only
description:
Per ADR-0019 decision 6: client-local focus pointer determines which TerminalId all non-prefix-handled input is addressed to. The driver currently has a single TerminalId hardcoded in the attach handshake; this ticket generalizes it.

Scope:
- The InputEvent → INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/INPUT_PASTE wire envelopes populate terminal_id from LayoutState.focus (set by phux-4li.5).
- Prefix-key handling happens BEFORE focus routing: if the resolver returns a Partial (prefix sequence in progress) or a ResolvedAction, the bytes are consumed by the TUI; otherwise they pass through to the focused pane's INPUT_KEY frame.
- Mouse: a click in pane A's Rect updates focus to A's TerminalId AND forwards the INPUT_MOUSE to A. A click on a divider is a no-op in v0.1 (drag-to-resize deferred per DESIGN.md §7).
- Focus events (terminal-window focus gained/lost) broadcast to the focused pane only.

NOT in scope:
- Drag-on-border to resize (DESIGN.md §7, not yet a phux-4li ticket).
- Per-pane mouse-mode override (set-pane mouse off).
- Copy-mode.

Acceptance:
- With two panes split vertically, focused on right: typing 'echo hi' lands in the right pane only.
- Clicking in the left pane shifts focus; subsequent typing lands in the left pane.
- The prefix chord ('C-b' or configured) is still captured by the TUI, not forwarded.
- cargo nextest run -p phux-client green with routing unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: All non-prefix input addressed to focused pane's TerminalId. Mouse click in a pane focuses it. Prefix not forwarded. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Landed in be740c7. Input routing now reads layout_state.focus.as_ref() (canonical) for INPUT_KEY/PASTE/FOCUS forwarding; drops with debug log if focus is None (no panic). New pure seam: attach::multi_pane::route_mouse_event(layout, viewport, mouse) -> RouteDecision{Pane{target, pane_x, pane_y, focus_changed} | DividerNoOp | NoFocus}. Click-to-focus updates layout_state.focus + clears predict overlay. Divider clicks no-op (drag-to-resize deferred). 6 unit tests on the pure seam.

title: Keybind action dispatch: split-h/v, focus-direction, kill-pane wired through phux-nz4.3 resolver
description:
Per ADR-0019 decisions 1, 2, 6: wire the resolver's ResolvedAction (from phux-nz4.3) into actual TUI behavior. Five actions land in v0.1:

- split-pane direction=horizontal|vertical: client computes new tree (using phux-4li.3 ops), SPAWNs a new Terminal in the same Collection, SET_METADATAs the new tree (via phux-4li.2 frames). New pane gets focus.
- focus-direction direction=up|down|left|right: calls LayoutState::focus_direction; updates client-local focus pointer; triggers re-render.
- kill-pane target=. (focused): SPAWN_KILL the Terminal (or TERMINAL_CLOSED arrives naturally when the PTY exits); on TERMINAL_CLOSED, prune the leaf from the tree and SET_METADATA. If only one pane left, single-pane fallback.
- resize-pane direction amount: walks up the tree from focused, finds the first interior split whose dir matches the action direction, adjusts ratio by amount/total_cells_along_axis. Per ADR-0019 decision 5: no-op + beep if a child would drop below 2 cols. SET_METADATA.
- next-pane / previous-pane: depth-first traversal cycling focus.

Scope:
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/actions.rs (new) dispatching ResolvedAction → effect.
- Wire into the driver's input handling loop (between resolver match and Terminal::vt_write).
- On METADATA_CHANGED for our layout key, re-GET and reconcile.

NOT in scope:
- Input routing to focused (phux-4li.6 — note: this ticket DOES update the focus pointer but does NOT route bytes; that's the sibling ticket).
- SIGWINCH (phux-4li.7).
- Configurable bindings beyond what nz4.3 already supports.

Acceptance:
- prefix + | splits vertical; new shell appears.
- prefix + h/j/k/l moves focus visibly.
- prefix + x kills focused; layout reflows.
- All actions trigger SET_METADATA and are visible to other attached clients within one round-trip.
- cargo nextest run -p phux-client green with action-dispatch unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: split-h/v, focus-direction, kill-pane, resize-pane, next/prev-pane actions dispatch correctly. SET_METADATA fires on layout-changing actions. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Landed in 78d224d. New attach::actions module with pure-function dispatch (apply_split/apply_kill/apply_focus/apply_resize/apply_next_pane/apply_previous_pane). Driver wires HELLO L3 layer + GetMetadata/SubscribeMetadata on attach + MetadataValue/MetadataChanged reconcile + dispatcher. Resolver action surface is string-based BTreeMap<String,toml::Value> (not enum). Five user-visible action names recognized. KNOWN GAP: split-pane and kill-pane warn+bell because SPAWN/TerminalClosed wire frames do not exist yet — see follow-up ticket. 19 unit tests on actions.rs all green.

title: Multi-pane render path: tile N Terminals, draw dividers, paint focus chrome
description:
Per ADR-0019 decisions 3 and 4: extend the attach driver to render multiple Terminals tiled per the layout tree, draw dividers between panes, and highlight the focused pane with heavy box-drawing on the adjacent divider edges.

Scope:
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs grows to hold N (Terminal, TerminalRenderer, RenderState) tuples instead of one. Keyed by TerminalId.
- New crates/phux-client/src/attach/multi_pane.rs (or inline in render.rs) that:
  - Takes a LayoutState (from phux-4li.3) and outer viewport dims.
  - Counts dividers from the tree topology (one row per Horizontal interior node along that axis path; one column per Vertical interior node).
  - Calls pane_rects((cols - h_dividers, rows - v_dividers)).
  - Paints each pane's Rect by handing the Rect's offset to that pane's TerminalRenderer.
  - Paints divider cells between panes using U+2500-U+257F. Light by default; heavy for divider edges adjacent to the focused pane.
- TerminalRenderer must support rendering into a sub-Rect of the outer viewport (offset + clip). If it doesn't today, extend it.
- Snapshot test: a known LayoutState renders to a known cell grid (insta snapshot of the painted output, or unit test that asserts divider cells are where expected).

NOT in scope:
- Layout-change action dispatch (phux-4li.5).
- Input routing to focused (phux-4li.6).
- Resize / SIGWINCH (phux-4li.7).
- Numeric pane tags / configurable border style (deferred per ADR-0019).

Acceptance:
- A two-pane vertical split renders correctly in 80x24: pane A in cols 0..39, divider at col 39, pane B in cols 40..79.
- Focused pane's adjacent divider edges use heavy box-drawing; inactive use light.
- cargo nextest run -p phux-client green.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-pane tiling renders N Terminals into the outer viewport with dividers; focused pane chrome distinct; cell budget correct per ADR-0019. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Landed in 983c18d. attach::multi_pane module with compute_layout/paint_dividers; PaneSlot{terminal, renderer} held in main_loop's panes HashMap keyed by TerminalId; layout_state field; render_at extension on TerminalRenderer for sub-Rect rendering. SIGWINCH not wired (deferred to follow-up); single-pane fast path preserved.

title: Client-side layout mirror: phux-client::layout module with binary split tree + CBOR envelope
description:
Per ADR-0019 decision 3: ship a client-side mirror of the binary split tree in crates/phux-client/src/layout.rs. The shape is the same as phux-protocol::wire::info::LayoutNode (imported, not re-derived); operations are re-implemented as free functions over the wire type because phux-client cannot depend on phux-core (ADR-0011).

Scope:
- New module crates/phux-client/src/layout.rs.
- Re-export phux-protocol::wire::info::LayoutNode, SplitDir.
- Free functions: split_at, kill_pane, focus_direction, pane_rects, replicating the algorithms from phux-core::window::Window. Same proptest invariants port over (every TerminalId appears as exactly one leaf, pane_rects tiles the rectangle, focus_direction is a partial function).
- struct LayoutState { tree: Option<LayoutNode>, focus: Option<TerminalId> }.
- CBOR envelope per ADR-0019 decision 1: {version: u8 = 1, root: LayoutNode, focus: TerminalId}. ciborium or serde_cbor — pick whichever has lighter deps; document in the PR.
- Encode/decode against the envelope; tests cover empty/single-pane/multi-split/edge cases.

NOT in scope:
- Wiring the mirror into driver.rs (phux-4li.4 owns that).
- L3 metadata GET/SET round-trip (phux-4li.2 owns the wire).

Acceptance:
- crate compiles; cargo nextest run -p phux-client green; proptests cover split/kill/focus/pane_rects invariants; CBOR envelope round-trips.
acceptance_criteria: phux-client::layout module exists with mirror of the binary split tree, CBOR envelope round-trips, proptest coverage matches phux-core::window. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Landed in aeac910. phux_client::layout module with re-exports of wire::info::{LayoutNode, SplitDir}, free functions split_at/kill_pane/focus_direction/pane_rects/leaves, LayoutState + ciborium v1 envelope. Two ergonomic deviations from phux-core::window: kill_pane returns Ok(None) for empty-tree (not Err(LastPane)); split_at takes new_pane: &TerminalId (clippy).

title: L3 metadata wire frames: discriminant allocation, encode/decode, server K/V store
description:
Per ADR-0019 decision 1: allocate the L3 metadata frame discriminant bytes (reserved as TBD in SPEC §7.4), implement encode/decode, and stand up a per-Collection K/V store in phux-server. This is the wire+server piece that the rest of phux-4li depends on.

Scope:
- Allocate discriminant bytes for GET_METADATA, SET_METADATA, DELETE_METADATA, LIST_METADATA, SUBSCRIBE_METADATA (C→S) and METADATA_CHANGED (S→C). Pick a contiguous block in SPEC §7.4's TBD range.
- Wire encode/decode in phux-protocol::wire::frame for each. Field layout: {scope: Scope, key: str, value: bytes} where Scope is a tagged union {Terminal{TerminalId}, Collection{CollectionId}, Global}.
- Server-side K/V: per-Collection HashMap<String, Vec<u8>> behind a Mutex in the existing state actor. Atomic SET; idempotent DELETE; LIST returns key names without values.
- SUBSCRIBE_METADATA tracks (scope, client_id) → keys; METADATA_CHANGED fanout on SET/DELETE only to subscribers in that scope.
- Insta snapshots for the new wire bytes (SPEC §16 conventions).
- Proptest round-trip for each frame.
- HELLO layer bit L3 must be advertised by server; clients declaring L3 in HELLO.layers can use these frames.

NOT in scope:
- CollectionId actually being populated by something (L2 lifecycle is still TBD; for v0.1 use a static 'default' CollectionId or implement just enough of L2 to have one — coordinate with whoever picks up L2 lifecycle next).
- CAS / optimistic locking on writes (ADR-0019 open question 2; defer).

Acceptance:
- HELLO.layers L3 bit honored end-to-end (server omits L3 frames to non-L3 consumers).
- cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol -p phux-server green with new tests.
- SPEC §7.4 updated with the allocated discriminant bytes.
acceptance_criteria: L3 metadata frames allocated and round-trip on the wire. Server stores per-Collection K/V and broadcasts METADATA_CHANGED. SPEC §7.4 reflects allocated bytes. cargo nextest green.
close_reason: Landed in d74beda. Allocated discriminants 0x50-0x54 (C->S), 0xD0 (METADATA_CHANGED). Scope tags 0x00/0x01/0x02. CollectionId(u32) newtype with DEFAULT_COLLECTION_ID=1 as v0.1 placeholder. HELLO grew trailing layers byte (additive per SPEC §6). METADATA_CHANGED carries value inline (departure from original SPEC §7.4 sketch). GET/LIST wire reply paths deferred — see follow-up ticket.

title: Sane-default config: new-pane CWD inheritance + spawn-on-attach + session naming
description:
Define and ship config knobs for new-pane defaults so phux behaves sensibly out of the box. Currently the only spawn-shaping config is `defaults.shell` (per DESIGN.md §4.2 minimal example).

New knobs (under `[defaults]`):
- `cwd-inheritance` — how new panes pick CWD. Values:
  - `inherit-focused` (default — matches tmux's behavior of starting in the same CWD as the pane you split from)
  - `home` — always `$HOME`
  - `session-root` — the CWD the session was created in
  - `last-cwd-per-window` — remember last CWD per window
- `spawn-on-attach` — when `phux` auto-creates a session, what to spawn. Default: `defaults.shell`.
- `session-name-template` — naming for auto-created sessions. Default: `"default"`. Supports `${cwd-basename}` substitution.
- `scrollback-lines` — per-Terminal scrollback cap. Default: 50000 (matches DESIGN.md §4.2 `history-limit`).

Server-side: SPAWN already carries a `cwd` argument. `inherit-focused` requires the client to know the focused pane's CWD, which means either (a) the server tracks PTY working dir per Terminal and exposes it on a getter frame, or (b) the client tracks it from shell OSC 7 announcements. (a) is cleaner; (b) is a fallback if no PTY support.

Depends on phux-4li (multi-pane) for new-pane to be a real user action. The `phux new` CLI path (under phux-k61) can use `session-name-template` and `spawn-on-attach` independently.

title: naked `phux` invocation — auto-create + attach to default session
description:
Per DESIGN.md §1: "today's binary ships only `attach` and `server`. The naked `phux` invocation errors with a usage hint instead of attaching to the last session." Wire it up.

Behavior:
- `phux` with no args:
  - If server is running and at least one session exists → attach to most-recently-focused session (or `default` if none has been focused yet).
  - If server is running but no sessions exist → create one (per config: `defaults.session-name-template` default `"default"` or `"${cwd-basename}"`) and attach.
  - If server is not running → auto-spawn (existing fork/poll loop from `phux attach`) and proceed as above.

Implementation notes:
- The auto-spawn fork/poll already lives in `phux attach`; this ticket reuses it. The new code is the dispatcher in `crates/phux/src/main.rs` plus session-resolution logic.
- "Most-recently-focused" needs a server-side hint or last-touched timestamp on Session. If that does not exist yet, fall back to "first session in insertion order" and file a follow-up.

Touches `defaults.session-name-template` config knob (see sibling ticket on sane-default config).
close_reason: Naked `phux` invocation shipped via PR. Follow-ups filed: phux-k61.2 (phux ls), phux-k61.3 (server CreateIfMissing), phux-k61.4 (most-recently-focused tracking).

title: [epic] CLI subcommands beyond attach/server (naked phux, new, ls, kill, …)
description:
Per DESIGN.md §1, the binary currently ships only `attach` and `server`. The rest of the table is "spec-only." To feel like tmux on the command line, ship the high-value subcommands.

Minimum daily-drive set (children at P2):
- naked `phux` — attach to default session, auto-create if none exists. Currently errors with usage hint per DESIGN.md §1 status note.
- `phux new [-s NAME] [-c CWD] [--] [COMMAND...]` — create a session.
- `phux ls` (alias `list-sessions`) — list sessions, `--json` available.
- `phux kill TARGET` — kill session/window/pane by DESIGN.md §3 selector.

Stretch (P3 children):
- `phux windows`, `phux panes`, `phux send`, `phux capture`, `phux config show|edit|path`, `phux messages`, `phux version`, `phux help`.

Conventions per DESIGN.md:
- All accept `--target`/`-t` consistently where applicable.
- Human-readable default; `--json` for `ls`/`windows`/`panes`/`capture`/`config show`/`server status`.
- Selector syntax: `.`, `name`, `name:N`, `name:N.M`, `name:tag`, `@N`, `=`.

The byc IPC + Registry primitives on the server already cover most of this; the work is a CLI dispatcher in `crates/phux/src/main.rs`, command-mode wire frames (or per-command frames if simpler), and selector resolution.

Success criteria:
- `phux` with no server running → auto-spawns server, auto-creates a session, attaches.
- `phux ls --json` returns valid JSON listing all sessions.
- `phux kill work` kills the named session and all its windows/panes.
- All command output is golden-tested.

title: [epic] Multi-pane TUI rendering + keybind→layout-action wiring
description:
The client currently renders a single pane. To feel like tmux, the TUI must render a window's layout tree as multiple panes (with borders), route input only to the focused pane, and wire keybind actions through to server-side layout ops.

Substrate mapping (per DESIGN.md §"How the TUI's user model maps to the substrate"):
- Window = TUI convention; layout-tree blob in L3 metadata, keyed by `phux.tui.layout/v1`.
- Pane = L1 Terminal; leaf of the layout tree.
- Active pane / focus = TUI convention, per-client.

Building blocks already in tree:
- `phux-byc.2` layout tree (split-h/v, kill-pane, focus-direction) — server-side ops exist.
- `phux-nz4.3` keybind resolver — maps KeyEvent → action name.
- `phux-q0e` per-consumer state sync — multiple Terminals already render per consumer.

Open design questions (worth an ADR or short design note before implementation):
- L3 layout metadata: where is it persisted (server-side under the Collection? per-client client-side?), and what is the canonical schema?
- Wire shape for layout ops: a new TUI_LAYOUT_OP request → server applies → broadcasts? Or strictly client-side composition over existing TERMINAL_* frames + a SPAWN to create new Terminals?
- Border drawing, focus highlight conventions (DESIGN.md is silent on this).
- Resize behavior under splits (proportional / fixed / pinned columns).

Success criteria:
- `prefix + |` (or configured key) splits current pane vertically; a new shell appears.
- `prefix + h/j/k/l` moves focus; focused pane has a visible indicator.
- `prefix + x` kills the focused pane; layout reflows.
- Host-terminal resize reflows all visible panes (depends on phux-4hp).
- Re-attach restores layout if persisted; otherwise opens a single pane.

This is gating work for daily-drive replacement of tmux. Hardest part of the daily-drive arc.

title: Revisit defensive .unwrap_or(Dirty::Full) once libghostty-rs set_dirty fix lands
description:
Tracking ticket for the user's local libghostty-rs fix at ../libghostty-rs.

## Upstream bug (diagnosed in phux-l0t)

libghostty-rs at `crates/libghostty-vt/src/render.rs:343`:

```rust
fn set<T>(&self, tag: ffi::RenderStateOption::Type, value: &T) -> Result<()> {
    let result = unsafe {
        ffi::ghostty_render_state_set(
            self.0.0.as_raw(),
            tag,
            std::ptr::from_ref(&value).cast(), // BUG: *const &T, drop the &
        )
    };
    ...
}
```

`std::ptr::from_ref(&value)` returns `*const &T` (pointer to the reference). C reads garbage. Fix: `std::ptr::from_ref(value).cast()`. Effect: `Snapshot::set_dirty(_)` poisons the next `Snapshot::dirty()` to return `Err(InvalidValue)`.

User is shipping the fix in their local clone of libghostty-rs.

## Phux-side cleanup when the fix lands (workspace Cargo.toml gets the new rev)

1. `crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs::synthesize_incremental` — the `.unwrap_or(Dirty::Full)` defensive default for `snapshot.dirty()` and `row.dirty()` becomes unnecessary. Restore the Clean / Partial fast paths so state-sync actually saves bytes instead of degrading to full-redraw post-`mark_synced`.

2. `crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs::mark_synced` — re-verify the doc comment that currently calls out the FFI bug; tighten/remove the workaround language.

3. `crates/phux-client/src/attach/render.rs::TerminalRenderer::render` — similar defensive default may exist; restore strict behavior.

4. `crates/phux-server/tests/phux_l0t_dirty_diagnosis.rs` — the diagnostic test from phux-l0t asserts the *broken* behavior so we get a loud failure when upstream fixes it. After updating libghostty-rs pin: this test will fail, which is the signal. Either invert the asserts (now testing the fixed contract) or delete the test (the contract is documented in libghostty-rs docs once fixed).

5. `crates/phux-server/tests/q0e_4_frame_ack.rs` (when shipped) — any tests that assert "post-mark_synced emission is non-empty" because of the FFI bug need updating; post-fix, post-mark_synced emissions may be empty (Clean) until next vt_write.

## How to detect it landed

The libghostty-rs pin in workspace Cargo.toml will change rev. Or the `phux_l0t_dirty_diagnosis.rs` tests will start failing on a CI run after a Cargo.lock update.

## Cross-refs

- phux-l0t (closed) — original diagnosis + repro test
- ADR-0018 Addendum (2026-05-26) — state-sync framework that depends on this
- PR #5 — diagnosis commit, doc comments to be revisited

title: FRAME_ACK handler → per-consumer dirty reset + last-acked CursorModeRef update
description:
Wire FRAME_ACK { terminal_id, seq } reception to the per-consumer RenderState dirty reset described in phux-q0e.B / ADR-0018 addendum.

Behavior:
- On FRAME_ACK from consumer C for terminal T with seq S:
  - If S > C.last_acked_seq for T: update C.last_acked_seq = S.
  - On C's RenderState for T: snapshot.set_dirty(Dirty::Clean); walk rows, row.set_dirty(false) per row that was clean at the *moment of emission for seq S*. (Careful: the RenderState may have absorbed new dirty bits since seq S was emitted. The correct invariant is "clear the bits that were set when we generated S, not the bits set now." Easiest impl: snapshot rows-emitted set per seq into a small per-consumer pending-acks map; on ACK, clear those specific row bits.)
  - Update C's last-acked CursorModeRef to the values emitted at S.
- If S <= C.last_acked_seq: drop the ACK (older or duplicate).

Loss tolerance is the consequence: if seq S is never acked, its dirty bits stay set on C's RenderState, so the next tick re-emits those rows in the new (larger) diff against the same older reference. No explicit retransmit logic.

Per spec-anchors memory: FRAME_ACK is defined in SPEC §7; consult before implementing.

Depends on phux-q0e.B (lifecycle) and phux-q0e.C (tick scheduler emits seq numbers).
close_reason: Shipped via PR #7; closes the phux-q0e epic state-sync loop. FRAME_ACK { terminal_id, seq } added on wire 0x21; TerminalActor::on_frame_ack walks per-consumer dirty cache eviction via SnapshotSynthesizer::mark_synced.

title: Tick scheduler + dirty pump (per-Terminal, fixed 33 Hz first-cut)
description:
Drive per-tick emission of incremental synthesis to all attached consumers of each Terminal.

First-cut: fixed 33 Hz (30 ms tick) per ADR-0018 / research note §"tick scheduler". RTT-adaptive interval is a follow-up (phux-q0e.E).

Shape:
- TerminalActor owns a tokio Interval (or LocalSet equivalent since RenderState is !Send).
- On tick: skip if no consumer's RenderState has any dirty (cheap pre-check via .dirty() per consumer).
- For each consumer with dirty != Clean: call incremental synthesizer (phux-q0e.A), produce TERMINAL_OUTPUT { terminal_id, seq, bytes }, send.
- Increment per-Terminal seq for each emission (per consumer? or shared? — wire spec says monotonic-per-terminal; can be shared seq counter advanced once per outbound TERMINAL_OUTPUT regardless of consumer, OR per-consumer seq spaces. Decide as part of this ticket; default to shared per spec-anchors memory).
- Do not block PTY ingestion on tick — the canonical Terminal vt_write proceeds whenever bytes arrive; tick is purely an emission timer.

Depends on phux-q0e.A and phux-q0e.B.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #6 (feat/q0e-3-tick-scheduler). Per-consumer SnapshotSynthesizer in ConsumerSyncState (decision (a)); per-consumer u64 seq starting at 1 (matches existing PTY-pump idiom and SPEC §8.1/§12 per-Terminal monotonic requirement, satisfied per-consumer); 33 Hz tokio Interval with MissedTickBehavior::Delay; try_send backpressure. Tests in crates/phux-server/tests/q0e_3_tick_scheduler.rs cover no-consumers, single-consumer, multi-consumer independent seq, and detach-mid-tick. CI green. Blocks phux-q0e.4 (FRAME_ACK handler).

title: Per-consumer RenderState lifecycle in TerminalActor (ATTACH allocates, DETACH frees)
description:
TerminalActor (per phux-28f) owns the canonical libghostty Terminal. For state-sync, it also owns one RenderState per attached consumer, plus the consumer's last-acked CursorModeRef snapshot.

Scope:
- ATTACH handler: allocate RenderState + initial CursorModeRef for the new consumer; emit TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT (existing from-empty path) as seq=0; reset that consumer's dirty bits on first FRAME_ACK.
- DETACH / consumer-disconnect: free the RenderState + state. Tracked via Drop on a per-consumer struct.
- Tick driver (phux-q0e.C) iterates consumers, calls render_state.update(terminal) per consumer, hands the snapshot to the incremental synthesizer (phux-q0e.A).
- FRAME_ACK from consumer C: clear C's dirty bits on its RenderState (set_dirty(Clean) on snapshot + per-row set_dirty(false)) and update C's CursorModeRef.

Blocks on phux-28f (TerminalActor placement decision — spawn_local vs. dedicated actor pattern). RenderState is !Send + !Sync per bd memory libghostty-send-sync, so per-consumer state must live on the same thread as the Terminal.

Estimate: ~150 LOC including ATTACH/DETACH paths.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #3; see ADR-0018 addendum.

title: Extend SnapshotSynthesizer to emit incremental diffs (skip-reset + per-row dirty consultation)
description:
Extend crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs::SnapshotSynthesizer with an incremental synthesis path. Today synthesize() always emits the from-empty case (DECSTR + ED 2 + CUP home + paint everything). State-sync needs the *delta* case.

Shape (per research/2026-05-26-state-sync-algorithm.md revised Dependencies §2):

1. Add synthesize_incremental(&mut self, terminal) -> Result<Bytes> (name TBD).
2. Skip the reset header.
3. Consult snapshot.dirty(): if Clean, return empty. If Full, fall back to synthesize() (full reset path). If Partial, walk rows.
4. For each row, skip if row.dirty() == false. Otherwise CUP to row + per-cell SGR-delta + grapheme — same inner loop as today's synthesize().
5. Cursor + mode bits: diff flat against per-consumer last-known state (caller passes in via small CursorModeRef struct? or holds in SnapshotSynthesizer? TBD as part of phux-q0e.B lifecycle work).
6. DO NOT call snapshot.set_dirty(Clean) at the end — caller (the tick driver) clears bits only on FRAME_ACK, not on emission. This is the loss-tolerance invariant.

Tests should mirror byc_6_1_attach_snapshot.rs's roundtrip pattern: vt_write sequence into a canonical Terminal, take incremental synthesis, apply via vt_write to a mirror Terminal, assert grids match.

Independent of phux-q0e.B (per-consumer lifecycle); can proceed in parallel.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #4: https://github.com/phall1/phux/pull/4. Note: dep on phux-l0t kept since the dirty()-reliability invariant still matters for the tick driver (phux-q0e.3) — this ticket only delivered the synthesizer extension. Force-closing because code is shipped.

title: TerminalId tagged-union shape (LOCAL/SATELLITE) per ADR-0016 §Decision
description: Deferred from Wave A. Convert TerminalId from u32 newtype to tagged union { LOCAL { id: u32 } tag=0, SATELLITE { host: SatelliteHost, id: u32 } tag=1 } per ADR-0016. Requires SatelliteHost type (see ADR-0007 reservation; may need to be defined as part of this). Wire-bytes change: 1-byte tag prefix on every TerminalId field. Snapshot tests will re-baseline. Wire version bump required. v0.1 servers construct LOCAL only; v0.1 decoders MUST accept SATELLITE tag and respond ERROR{UnsupportedSatelliteRoute} if not configured as federation hub.
close_reason: Landed in commit 0ac50bf via PR #11. TerminalId is now a tagged union (Local/Satellite) with 1-byte wire tag prefix. PROTOCOL_VERSION 0.1.0 → 0.2.0. SPEC.md updated with changelog row. 37 files / +585/-228. All snapshot tests rebaselined. just ci green on integrated main.

title: Wave C: SPEC.md restructured by tier (L1/L2/L3) per ADR-0015
description: After Wave A (rename) lands. Restructure SPEC.md so the message catalog is organized by tier. WindowId, SessionId-as-group-concept, LayoutNode, LAYOUT_CHANGED, FOCUS_CHANGED, WINDOW_OPENED/CLOSED/RENAMED leave the normative spec and become TUI L3 metadata conventions (documented in DESIGN.md or a TUI design doc). Conformance language becomes per-tier. CC_FRONTEND capability bit reclaimed (ADR-0017). Bump wire/spec version. §10 (sessions/windows/panes/layout) gets refactored or moved to DESIGN.md TUI conventions appendix.

WindowId/LAYOUT_CHANGED/FOCUS_CHANGED/WINDOW_*/LayoutNode demoted to non-normative SPEC §17 (TUI consumer conventions, L3 metadata-backed). New error codes: COLLECTION_NOT_FOUND, METADATA_KEY_NOT_FOUND, OUT_OF_TIER, UNSUPPORTED_SATELLITE_ROUTE. just check + rustdoc -D warnings green.

title: test-harness: Screen helper (libghostty oracle for client render bytes)
description: Build a Screen helper that uses libghostty_vt::Terminal as an oracle to parse client-rendered VT bytes into an inspectable grid. Enables ergonomic screen.row(0).contains('hi') assertions instead of regex-based VT byte stripping in tests.
close_reason: Closed

title: server: PTY EOF (shell 'exit') doesn't notify client — client freezes in alt-screen
description:
User repro: typed 'exit' in the pane's shell. Shell exits. Client TUI froze; no path back to the outer shell except Ctrl-b d (detach) or kill.

Current behavior: pane_actor.rs:598 handles PtyEvent::Eof by detaching the PTY-read branch (self.pty_rx = None) and reaping the child, then keeping the actor alive 'for snapshot/input drain'. The server never broadcasts a state change. runtime.rs only emits FrameKind::Detached when the *client* requests detach (line 590).

Desired behavior options:
(a) On last-pane-EOF in the attached session, server sends FrameKind::Detached so the client unwinds its alt-screen guard cleanly.
(b) Server sends a new PaneClosed frame; client picks another pane to focus, or sends Detach if none remain.
(c) Server keeps the empty pane in place but allows the user to respawn via a re-seed command.

(a) is the minimum viable behavior — matches tmux/screen 'session closed' UX. (b) is the long-term shape once windows/panes have richer lifecycle ops; tracks SPEC §10. Pick one as the immediate fix and file (b) follow-on if (a) ships first.

Also: confirm the PaneActor's 'keep alive for snapshot/input drain' comment still makes sense once we notify clients on EOF — a closed pane shouldn't accept new input, and snapshots of a dead pane have unclear semantics.
close_reason: Fixed in commit ad93cd7 on fix/phux-it8-eof-detach: PaneActor now fires an internal exit_notify oneshot on PTY EOF; the runtime's per-pane EOF watcher broadcasts FrameKind::Detached to attached clients and detaches them. Test: crates/phux-server/tests/eof_detach.rs (pty_eof_drives_detached_frame_to_attached_client). Multi-pane (phux-9gw) deferred via TODO.

title: client/server: app inside pane doesn't receive expected keys (e.g. htop 'q' to quit)
description:
User repro: ran phux attach demo, ran htop inside, pressed 'q' to quit — no effect. Likely root cause: phux advertises TERM=ghostty to the inner shell (pane_actor.rs:271). Ghostty's terminfo declares kitty keyboard protocol support, so apps that detect this can flip into CSI-u mode where every keypress (including 'q') becomes a CSI-u escape like \x1b[113;1u. Apps that don't natively understand kitty protocol (htop, less, many old TUIs) won't recognize the quit key.

Investigation needed:
- Trace the exact bytes phux's per-pane key encoder writes when 'q' is pressed inside htop (instrument crates/phux-server/src/input/key.rs around the Event::encode call).
- Check whether htop's terminal-init turns on kitty modes (CSI > 1 u). If so, the encoder will produce CSI-u bytes; if not, plain ASCII.
- Decide policy: (a) advertise TERM=xterm-256color until ghostty's kitty support is mature in phux's path; (b) make ghostty terminfo correct for our path; (c) downgrade kitty protocol when the inner app hasn't requested it (which should already be the case — confirm).

Related: phux-19e (smarter client stdin parsing) is about the opposite direction. This ticket is about server-side encoding mismatching the inner app's expectations.
close_reason: 3120f40 — fix(server): default TERM=xterm-256color to unbreak htop quit

title: feat(tooling): dhat-heap feature for binary heap profiling
description: Add opt-in dhat-heap cargo feature on the phux binary crate to produce dhat-heap.json on shutdown. Mirrors the just-landed tokio-console + samply tooling. Catches steady-state allocator pressure on PTY pump + snapshot synthesis paths.
close_reason: Closed

title: feat(server): tokio-console feature for live actor introspection
description: Add opt-in tokio-console cargo feature on phux-server with tracing_subscriber bootstrap (stderr fmt + EnvFilter from RUST_LOG, default phux=info,warn). Layer ConsoleLayer when feature enabled. Initialize from binary entry. Requires --cfg tokio_unstable via RUSTFLAGS. High-ROI observability for actor system with broadcast channels (PANE_OUTPUT).

title: feat(tooling): samply CPU profiling — just profile recipe + [profile.profiling]
description: Add lightweight, reproducible CPU profiling via samply. Workspace profile.profiling (inherits release, debug=1, strip=none) + just profile recipe that builds + invokes samply record. samply is cargo install (not workspace dep); recipe checks command -v samply and prints install hint.
close_reason: Closed

title: SnapshotSynthesizer: distinguish wide-cell tails from blank cells via cell.wide()
description:
Surfaced in 460faeb. crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs::synthesize emits a space for any cell where cell.graphemes().is_empty() — covers both genuinely-blank cells AND wide-cell tails (the right half of '東', emoji, etc.). Affects snapshot fidelity for wide-character content.

Fix: use libghostty's CellIteration::raw_cell() then Cell::wide() to discriminate. When wide() indicates a tail position, skip emission (the base grapheme already advanced the cursor past it). Otherwise emit space.

Test: round-trip a snapshot containing CJK + emoji through Synthesizer → fresh Terminal → assert grid equivalence. Use the existing synthesizer_round_trips_via_libghostty test as the pattern.

Blocks: nothing critical, but affects byc.8's PANE_SNAPSHOT correctness for any pane content with wide graphemes.
close_reason: Landed in commit e8b92e8 via PR #19 (with the underlying grid.rs fix already on main in 5c0cb82). PR added CJK+emoji round-trip test (synthesizer_round_trips_cjk_and_emoji) hardening regression coverage via CellIteration::raw_cell()?.wide() = CellWide::SpacerTail.

title: RenderState::update second-call diagnosis (deferred from 460faeb)
description:
Wave-2 agent's regression test (call render() twice in a row, assert Dirty::Clean on second) hit Error::InvalidValue from libghostty. Agent dropped the test rather than diagnose. Production paths (server SnapshotSynthesizer + client PaneRenderer) work fine — they each call snapshot.set_dirty(Dirty::Clean) between updates. The contract IS clear from libghostty source: Snapshot holds &mut RenderState; you can update again after Snapshot drops.

InvalidValue comes from .dirty().and_then(|v| v.try_into().map_err(|_| Error::InvalidValue)) — FFI returned a Dirty value outside {Clean, Partial, Full}. Likely the test was missing the set_dirty(Clean) reset between updates, OR libghostty returns an out-of-band Dirty value in a specific scenario the agent didn't characterize.

Tiny investigation: reconstruct the failing test scenario, find the actual cause, either fix the test or file a libghostty upstream issue if the FFI is returning an unmodeled value.
notes: Re-prioritized 2026-05-26 from P3 → P2: this becomes load-bearing for state-sync per ADR-0018 addendum. State-sync's tick driver calls snapshot.dirty() per consumer per tick on the same RenderState across many updates. The FFI InvalidValue case the deferred test hit (Dirty value outside {Clean, Partial, Full}) must be either characterized (so the synthesizer can guard against it) or fixed upstream before phux-q0e.A ships. See phux-q0e (epic) for the dependent work.
close_reason: Diagnosed via PR #5: Outcome B (upstream FFI bug in libghostty-vt rev 31d1f70 at Snapshot::set — passes *const *const T to C instead of *const T). Calling Snapshot::set_dirty(_) poisons the next update's dirty() read with InvalidValue, value-agnostic. Diagnostic test in crates/phux-server/tests/phux_l0t_dirty_diagnosis.rs pins the contract. Existing .unwrap_or(Dirty::Full) workarounds in client renderer and server synthesize_incremental implement strict over-emission, preserving ADR-0018 loss-tolerance. Dirty::Clean/Partial fast paths are dead code in any pipeline that calls mark_synced until upstream is fixed. Upstream issue to file as follow-up.

title: phux-server::downsample: handle image protocols + keyboard protocol gating + OSC 8 stripping
description:
Follow-up to ADR-0013 byte-stream rewriter landing. The v0 rewrite_bytes() in phux-server::downsample handles truecolor SGR -> indexed256/16 only. SPEC §6.2 requires additional gating per client caps:

- Image protocols: drop or transform sixel (DCS) / kitty graphics (APC G) / iTerm2 (OSC 1337) when the client did not advertise the matching ImageProtocol bit. The OSC/DCS/APC passthrough is permissive today; needs a capability-gated drop path.
- Kitty keyboard protocol: APC keyboard-reply sequences must be stripped from the outbound stream for clients that did not negotiate the matching kbd_protocols bit. The canonical Terminal still processes them locally.
- OSC 8 hyperlinks: strip when ClientCapabilities.hyperlinks == false.
- ITU-style colon separators: the rewriter currently only recognises ';'. tmux handles both 'CSI 38;2;R;G;B m' and 'CSI 38:2::R:G:B m'; add the colon variant for parity.

Scope: code only (crates/phux-server/src/downsample.rs + tests). No SPEC changes needed — §6.2 already mandates the behavior; this is implementation catch-up.

title: SPEC cleanup: §12 flow control + §2 terminology + Appendix B + §11.1 ADR ref + §6.2 RenderingMode
description:
Post-ADR-0013 SPEC inconsistencies surfaced by the §8 rewrite agent (commit 2071f8f). Out-of-scope of that commit; needs a single small cleanup pass:

1. §12 Flow control still references PANE_DIFF and frame_id-based FRAME_ACK. Update to PANE_OUTPUT and per-pane seq.
2. §2 Terminology 'Frame' definition still uses frame_id. Reword to: 'frame: a server-emitted PANE_OUTPUT carrying a contiguous batch of VT bytes for one pane, identified by a monotonically increasing per-pane seq.'
3. Appendix B Reserved ranges still mentions DiffOp tag allocation guidance. Drop or replace with NamedKey/ErrorCode-only guidance.
4. §11.1 'What is deliberately absent' cross-references ADR-0002 (superseded). Repoint to ADR-0013 or to the appropriate post-supersession ADR.
5. §6.2 RenderingMode { Diff | VtReplay } kept for forward-compat but deprecated in prose; a future major-version SPEC removes the field entirely. File a separate v0.2 ticket if/when major bump happens.

Single-commit cleanup. Scope: SPEC.md only. No code touched.

Verified in main: §2 Frame term re-anchored on per-Terminal seq (line 95); §6.2 RenderingMode deprecated in prose (line 318-322); §11.1 ADR ref points at ADR-0013 (line 1421-1423); §12 flow control uses TERMINAL_OUTPUT + per-Terminal seq (line 1427+); Appendix B explicitly drops DiffOp guidance (line 1898-1900). title: phux-protocol: add ERROR FrameKind variant per SPEC §14

## Required Skills

design:
Add to crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/frame.rs:
- pub const TYPE_ERROR: u8 = 0xC1;
- An ErrorCode enum (or just newtype u16) matching SPEC §14's code table (SESSION_NOT_FOUND=102, plus the others enumerated in SPEC §14).

title: phux-server: handle ATTACH frame and respond with ATTACHED + PANE_SNAPSHOT
description:
The server's per-client task in crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs::handle_client today only handles FrameKind::Ping. The match arm for FrameKind::Attach / FrameKind::Detach / FrameKind::Input* falls through to a debug log and the connection sits idle. byc.6 (the integration tests) is blocked on this handler existing.

Under ADR-0013 the response sequence to ATTACH is bytes-on-wire:

1. Resolve target -> SessionId via ServerState.
2. Allocate per-client mpsc + spawn writer task draining into the UnixStream.
3. Build SessionSnapshot from the registry; send FrameKind::Attached { snapshot, initial_client_id }.
4. For each pane in the focused window: call phux_server::grid::SnapshotSynthesizer::synthesize(&terminal) to produce { cols, rows, vt_replay_bytes }, then send FrameKind::PaneSnapshot { pane_id, cols, rows, vt_replay_bytes, scrollback_bytes: None } per SPEC §8.4 / §13.
5. Subsequent live output flows as FrameKind::PaneOutput { pane_id, seq, bytes } -- the per-pane PTY pump + downsample::rewrite_bytes path that byc.5 covers.

On FrameKind::Detach: send Detached and drop the writer.

On AttachError::UnknownSession: send an ERROR frame with code SESSION_NOT_FOUND (102 per SPEC §14). Requires the ERROR variant — see follow-up phux-byc.9.

Acceptance: byc.6 tests #1, #2, #5 must pass once this and the ERROR-variant ticket land.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,ipc,libghostty
design:
On receiving FrameKind::Attach { target, viewport, request_scrollback, scrollback_limit_lines }:
1. Resolve target → SessionId via ServerState (AttachTarget::ByName lookup is implemented; ById / Last / CreateIfMissing per SPEC §13).
2. Allocate a per-client mpsc channel and call ServerState::attach(client_id, name, tx).
3. Spawn a writer task draining the mpsc into the UnixStream's write half.
4. Build a SessionSnapshot from the registry and send FrameKind::Attached { snapshot, initial_client_id }.
5. For each pane in the session's active window, send FrameKind::PaneSnapshot per SPEC §13 attach sequence.

On FrameKind::Detach: send FrameKind::Detached, then drop the writer (which closes the socket on the server side).

On AttachError::UnknownSession: send an ERROR frame with code SESSION_NOT_FOUND (102 per SPEC §14). Requires the ERROR variant — see follow-up ticket.

Acceptance: byc.6 tests #1, #2, #5 must pass once this and the ERROR-variant ticket land.

DETACH responds with Detached (line 589). UnknownSession/AlreadyAttached return ERROR frames. Wire-level coverage in crates/phux-server/tests/attach_lifecycle.rs and byc_6_1_attach_snapshot.rs. INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/INPUT_FOCUS dispatch is a separate gap tracked under a sibling impl ticket filed 2026-05-26.

title: Conform DiffOp to SPEC §8.1/§8.5: cursor + modes as PANE_DIFF fields, not ops
description:
SPEC §8.1 defines `PANE_DIFF` as carrying `cursor: CursorState`, `modes: PaneModes`, and `base_frame_id`/`revision` as **struct fields**, not as entries in the `ops` list. SPEC §8.5 is explicit on the rationale: "pulling them out into separate messages would increase wire chatter for no benefit." The current implementation models cursor as `DiffOp::CursorMove { row, col }` (op-byte 0x03) and `DiffOp::CursorStyle { visible, shape, blink }` (op-byte 0x04), and the `PaneDiff` frame body has no cursor / modes / base_frame_id / revision fields.

Why now: scope is small (one server example, one client mirror, a handful of tests). As more compute/apply/render code lands, the op-stream shape will calcify and the migration will be harder. Wire format is breaking — no external consumer yet — so do it before anyone latches.

Surgical edit list:
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/op.rs: delete `DiffOp::CursorMove` and `DiffOp::CursorStyle` variants
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/cursor.rs (new): re-home `CursorState`, `CursorShape`, add `PaneModes` bitset
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/diff.rs: delete op-byte 0x03/0x04 encode/decode + proptest strategy entries
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/frame.rs: `PaneDiff` gains `base_frame_id: u64`, `cursor: CursorState`, `modes: PaneModes`, `revision: u8`
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/decode.rs: decode the new fields
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/field.rs: add field-ID constants for new PaneDiff fields
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/compute.rs: stop emitting cursor ops; return cursor + modes alongside ops
- crates/phux-client/src/mirror/apply.rs: drop CursorMove/CursorStyle match arms; consume cursor+modes from PaneDiff
- crates/phux-client/src/mirror/state.rs: track modes (or pipe through)
- crates/phux-server/examples/diff_spike.rs and one_pane.rs: update to new compute_diff signature
- Tests: regenerate snapshots, append new cursor-field + modes round-trip tests, replace existing CursorMove/CursorStyle tests
close_reason: Landed in fd89d93dc712c4d46aee8f5066ffaa846f59e7d8. CursorState + PaneModes + base_frame_id + revision fields added to PaneDiff per SPEC §8.5; op-stream cursor ops deleted. New diff_spike md5: b84a5890eda2277734faad33b7411c74. Follow-up phux-bxa filed for MouseProtocol/MouseEncoding enums.

title: Wire ColorSupport through HELLO/ClientCapabilities per SPEC §6.1/§6.2

Follow-up to phux-2li. The substrate (`ColorSupport` enum, `downsample` fn,
